name: Chocolate Pots De Creme
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the German chocolate finely.
2. In a small nonmetal bowl, stir together light cream, chopped chocolate, sugar, and salt. Cook uncovered on high for about 1 minute or until the chocolate is melted, stirring after 30 seconds.
3. Stir about half of the hot mixture into the beaten egg yolk. Return all to the bowl and mix well.
4. Cook uncovered on medium for 1 to 2 minutes or until thickened, stirring every 15 seconds.
5. Stir in vanilla. Pour into a pot de creme cup or 6-ounce custard cup. Cover and chill for several hours or until firm. Garnish with whipped cream if desired.
NOTE:
Selecting the Right Chocolate: You'll find three basic types of chocolate in the baking supplies department of most supermarkets—semisweet chocolate, unsweetened chocolate, and sweet chocolate. Semisweet chocolate is made from chocolate that is slightly sweetened with sugar. Unsweetened chocolate is the original baking or cooking chocolate and has no added flavorings or sugar. Sweet chocolate, such as German cooking chocolate used in this Chocolate Pots de Creme recipe, is chocolate mixed with sugar and sometimes additional cocoa butter or flavorings.
